name: Pulumi
what_it_does: Infrastructure as Code platform that lets you define cloud resources using real programming languages (Python, TypeScript, Go, C#, Java, YAML) instead of domain-specific languages. Provides CLI, Pulumi Cloud for state management and collaboration, and an Automation API for embedding IaC directly into application programs.
use_cases: Provisioning and tearing down cloud infrastructure programmatically from agents via the Automation API
Self-service infrastructure portals that spin up environments on demand
Multi-cloud resource management from a single Python or TypeScript codebase
GitOps and CI/CD infrastructure pipelines triggered via Pulumi Cloud REST API
Dynamic stack creation with per-tenant or per-PR ephemeral environments
not_for: Teams deeply invested in Terraform/OpenTofu HCL who don't want to migrate
Simple one-off resource creation where the console or CLI suffices
Environments where the Pulumi CLI binary cannot be installed on the agent host
best_when: An agent needs to provision, update, or destroy cloud infrastructure programmatically and you want to embed IaC logic directly in application code using the Automation API rather than shelling out to a CLI.
avoid_when: Your team has standardized on Terraform and the migration cost exceeds the benefit; Terraform still has a larger provider ecosystem.

auth.notes: Pulumi Cloud uses personal or organization access tokens (PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN env var). OIDC federation supported for CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab, CircleCI) to eliminate long-lived secrets. Tokens are org-scoped but not fine-grained per resource. Self-managed backends (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) use native cloud credentials.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Individual: free forever, 1 user, unlimited resources, up to 150,000 state updates/year
pricing.paid_tiers: Team Starter: $50/month for 3 users; Team: $500/month for 10 users; Enterprise: custom pricing with SAML SSO, audit logs, self-hosted option
pricing.notes: The OSS Pulumi engine and Automation API are fully free. Pulumi Cloud is the managed backend for state and collaboration — you can also self-host state in any object storage for free. The Automation API is not gated behind paid tiers.

performance.rate_limits: Pulumi Cloud REST API: not publicly documented; deployments are async and queued. Automation API is in-process with no network overhead for the orchestration layer.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Pulumi surfaces provider errors (from AWS, GCP, Azure SDKs) which vary widely in quality. Pulumi-own errors (state conflicts, auth failures, lock errors) are clear and structured. The Automation API wraps failures in typed exceptions with stack and update context. Stack traces can be verbose.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: IaC model is inherently idempotent — re-running a stack update converges to desired state. Pulumi diffs current state against desired state before making changes. The Automation API exposes Up(), Preview(), Refresh(), Destroy() with consistent declarative semantics.
known_agent_gotchas: Automation API requires the Pulumi CLI binary installed on the agent host — it is not a pure Python/Go library
Stack state locking prevents concurrent deployments on the same stack — handle lock errors with backoff and retry
Deployments are asynchronous — agents must poll update status endpoint or use webhooks, not assume completion
Provider credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS, etc.) must be in agent environment — not passed through Pulumi Cloud
Secrets written to stack config are encrypted and cannot be read back via API — write-only for secret values
Stack outputs are only available after successful deployment — querying outputs mid-deploy returns stale values

security_score_components.security_notes: Pulumi ESC (Environments, Secrets, Config) provides first-class secrets management with integrations to Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and Azure Key Vault. OIDC federation eliminates long-lived credentials in CI. Secrets encrypted at rest in Pulumi Cloud. Access tokens support org-scoping but lack fine-grained resource-level permissions.
